candidate_mechanism.title: Structure Formation Detail

equations.structure_driver: Psi(f_{t+1}, A_t) = c_c * C(f_{t+1}) + c_d * D_t + c_r * R_t + c_b * B_region - c_p * P_t
equations.region_bias: B_region = u_route * p_route + u_multi * p_multi + u_abs * p_abs

interpretation.coactivation: newly co-activated features propose local edges and short path reinforcements
interpretation.route_mismatch: task demand that current topology cannot serve pushes additional bridge formation
interpretation.region_bias: the same plasticity rule becomes routing-heavy or abstraction-heavy by changing regional priors
interpretation.prune_pressure: slow memory turns unused or unstable edges into prune pressure rather than permanent growth
interpretation.full_update: effective topology grows when coactivation and route demand agree, and shrinks when slow stabilization marks excess structure

candidate_mechanism.core_claim: Effective network structure is not a fixed scaffold. It is a demand-shaped topology that grows from feature coactivation, route mismatch pressure, and regional priors, then gets pruned by slow stabilization.

project_readout.summary: P6B is positive only if the structure term can be expanded into a concrete topology-formation law, rather than left as an unspecified mid-timescale update.
project_readout.next_question: If P6B holds, the next step is to detail the feature-extraction term with the same level of mechanistic explicitness.
